task: Launch-quality advanced task: Satellite Radiator Contamination Degradation Forecast. Reality context: an on-orbit thermal team sees rising component temperatures after thruster plume events. Embedded packet: metric map: metric1=absorptivity_shift, metric2=emissivity_shift, metric3=hot_case_margin_C, metric4=mission_month; records: R1 absorptivity_shift=0.09 emissivity_shift=0.007 hot_case_margin_C=14.4 mission_month=58; R2 absorptivity_shift=0.098 emissivity_shift=0.018 hot_case_margin_C=11.8 mission_month=60; R3 absorptivity_shift=0.093 emissivity_shift=-0.088 hot_case_margin_C=7.7 mission_month=47; R4 absorptivity_shift=0.016 emissivity_shift=0.026 hot_case_margin_C=5.1 mission_month=5; limit_absorptivity_shift=0.101; limit_emissivity_shift=-0.097; limit_hot_case_margin_C=18; decision_budget_usd=92500; sensitivity_shift_pct=5; hard_threshold_record_count=1; removed_context="guidance required separating verified evidence from unresolved findings". Rules and constraints: Fit a simple linear or monotone relationship from the embedded rows only; flag records whose residual exceeds the supplied tolerance or reverses expected direction. Options or recommendations cannot use external measurements, specialized licensed software, or hidden assumptions; all calculations must use only the embedded packet, and any contradictory or under-supported record must be marked needs_review. Required work: forecast whether attitude or heater changes are needed; convert the packet into an auditable evidence table, cite the governing values, and list unresolved assumptions. Deliver radiator_degradation_model.xlsx + ops_recommendation.md with formulas or executable logic, record-level citations, validation checks, sensitivity notes, and a final recommendation table suitable for expert review.
